title: X-Wing Rogue Squadron 11

'''''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 11''', also labeled as '''"Battleground: Tatooine, Chapter 3"''', is the eleventh issue in the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' series of comics. It was released on [[August 14]], [[1996]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Jabba the Hutt is dead, and in the ensuing scramble for power, a secret Imperial weapons cache could be the key to victory. But only one being alive knows the location of the hidden weapons cache -- Firith Olan. While that makes Firith a very powerful being, it also makes him a prime target. In a desperate race to uncover the missing weapons, as the criminals converge to fill the role of the dead Hutt, Wedge and Rogue Squadron find themselves smack in the middle of ''Battleground: Tatooine''!''

==Plot summary==
As [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] engages [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter|TIE fighters]] and [[Firith Olan]]'s [[TIE/IN interceptor|TIE Interceptors]], the discover that the recently arrived Imperials are only concerned with targeting Olan's forces. Rogue Squadron decides not to risk intercepting Olan's [[Firith Olan's freighter|freighter]] and retreat to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] surface, while the Imperials, commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Marl Semtin/Legends|Marl Semtin]] of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Harrow]]'', want Olan alive, deciding to intercept him on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]]. 

The Rogues [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] and [[Elscol Loro]] accompany [[Winter Celchu|Winter]] to capture Olan on Ryloth as well. Arriving at the Kala'uun starport, the Rogues give starport master [[Koh'shak]] a "docking fee" of exorbitant gifts. Elsewhere, Olan meets with [[Cazne'olan]], head of the Olan clan, who wants to give up Olan to either the Empire or the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]]. Cazne'olan orders a contest judged by Koh'shak to decide which faction will win Olan, where Antilles and Winter are pitted against the imposing Imperial operatives [[Sixtus Quin]] and [[Septaas]]. 

The four combatants are transported to Ryloth's hazardous surface to capture a golden globe some distance away. Each are given a traditional lyaer-tsa and are told to only inflict non-lethal damage on their opponents. As the contest begins, Quin and Septaas strike the Rogues and gain a head start, but Septaas is impaled by a wild [[Lylek/Legends|lylek]]. Antilles and Winter battle the creature, where despite Winter's protests, Antilles saves her life at the expense of allowing Quin to capture the globe. At that moment, a Ryloth [[heat storm]] begins, incinerating the three remaining combatants. 

The four combatants awaken and realize that the contest was a simulation. Quin regards the Rogues well, satisfied with their demonstration of honor. Elsewhere, Firith Olan tortures [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]], telling Fortuna of his plan to trade him for his own freedom. Olan is confronted by a shadowed figure with a blaster and escapes imprisonment. 

Koh'shak is ordered to close the portcullis, as Olan has escaped, apparently aided by the Imperials. Koh-shak ignores the request, keeping the port open for Imperial entry, as Captain Semtin had gifted him a tunic made of [[koolach silk]]. Quin is insulted by the lack of honor exhibited by his superiors and defects, asking Antilles if he will give himself and his deserted Imperial allies a chance to do what is right. 

Hauling the Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] off of Ryloth, Celchu asks Winter if she would have saved Antilles in the contest. Winter asserts that she would have prioritized the globe, as that was what the mission dictated. Celchu proposes that Winter's name came from her cold disposition, or the "ice [[Water/Legends|water]]" in her veins. Asserting that he needs no other surprises when they get back to Tatooine, Fortuna in his [[B'omarr brain walker|walker droid]] lurks above them in the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]].
